growth_records.medium: MBSY medium (modified Brock's basal salt + yeast extract, sulfur-free)
growth_records.cite: Sakai et al. Insight into the symbiotic lifestyle of DPANN archaea revealed by cultivation and genome analyses. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115449119. [PMC8784108]
growth_records.snippet: The highest ARM-1 cell density was observed at 55 °C (∼5.5 × 10^9 copies of 16S rRNA gene per milliliter), while the shortest doubling time was observed at 70 °C (8.4 h; Dataset S2).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (8.4 h at 70°C, pH 3.0, aerobic, MBSY medium) directly reported in main text as the shortest observed across a 40-80°C growth range for the ARM-1/Metallosphaera sp. AS-7 obligate coculture. Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time = 0.6931/8.4 = 0.0825 h-1. ARM-1 (Ca. Micrarchaeota, DPANN superphylum) requires the host archaeon AS-7 for growth (obligate epibiotic symbiont); no growth occurs without host per the paper.
